evidence: Genuine opinions 6/10 — Takes some stances (Ala Moana over Waikiki, book early) but many claims are generic travel-blog safe.

suggestedPatch.target: Every good Oahu group trip mixes beaches, food, chill time, and one day out of the main city.
suggestedPatch.before: Every good Oahu group trip mixes beaches, food, chill time, and one day out of the main city.
suggestedPatch.after: Skip the cultural stuff on day one—jet-lagged friends fight over museums but agree on beach and beer.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Genuine opinions" scored 6/10: Takes some stances (Ala Moana over Waikiki, book early) but many claims are generic travel-blog safe.

evidence: Unexpected phrasings 6/10 — 'One organiser, seven opinions, 1000 unread messages' is good, but much reverts to predictable listicle cadence.

suggestedPatch.target: Friend groups don't need more platitudes, you need dates, places, and backup backups.
suggestedPatch.before: Friend groups don't need more platitudes, you need dates, places, and backup backups.
suggestedPatch.after: Friend groups don't need vibes—they need a car, a Hanauma confirmation email, and one person willing to be the jerk about timing.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Unexpected phrasings" scored 6/10: 'One organiser, seven opinions, 1000 unread messages' is good, but much reverts to predictable listicle cadence.

evidence: Quotable sentences 5/10 — The 'moment someone suggests Hanauma Bay' line is quotable, but most sentences are too transactional to share.

suggestedPatch.target: Stop trying to optimise every hour, here's a daily plan that keeps everyone (mostly) happy and hits the spots your group will actually talk about.
suggestedPatch.before: Stop trying to optimise every hour, here's a daily plan that keeps everyone (mostly) happy and hits the spots your group will actually talk about.
suggestedPatch.after: Stop optimising every hour—the group that schedules lunch loses the trip.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Quotable sentences" scored 5/10: The 'moment someone suggests Hanauma Bay' line is quotable, but most sentences are too transactional to share.

evidence: Intro earns attention 3/10 — Buried under breadcrumb clutter, TL;DR, byline, and a generic 'shouldn't be chaos' line before any hook lands.
